title: OOPS pillars & SOLID principles + DSA Heap
focus: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP — pick the one you violate most and refactor.

items.label: K Closest Points TO Origin
interviewQuestions: Why is a heap the right structure? Could a balanced BST or sorted list work — why is heap better?
Explain the heap-of-k pattern: keep size k, push new, pop if over k. What's the resulting complexity?
What does the comparator look like, and how would you tweak it to flip min/max behaviour?

items.label: Kth Largest Element IN AN Array
interviewQuestions: Compare heap (O(n log k)), sort (O(n log n)), quickselect (O(n) avg) — when does each fit?
What's quickselect's worst case, and how do you avoid it (median-of-medians, randomization)?

items.label: Encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction
interviewQuestions: Walk through the four pillars of OOP with code examples.

items.label: SOLID: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP
interviewQuestions: Walk through each SOLID principle with a real example.
Which SOLID principle do you violate most often, and why?

items.label: Common OO design mistakes (god class, anemic models, deep inheritance)
interviewQuestions: Spot a god class — what would you refactor it into?
Why are deep inheritance hierarchies usually a smell, and what do you reach for instead?
